evidenceRows.magnitudeRaw: 132279 stars
evidenceRows.notes: Confirmed via GitHub API. 132,279 stars, 11,510 forks, 878 subscribers. Repo created 2026-02-03, last push 2026-06-12. Exceptional growth rate for a ~4-month-old repo.

evidenceRows.magnitudeRaw: 11510 forks, 102 commits, 80 open issues, 1 active PR
evidenceRows.notes: Active maintenance confirmed: 102 commits on main, MIT license, Shell language, 264KB size. Repo description: 'Skills for Real Engineers. Straight from my .claude directory.'

evidenceRows.magnitudeRaw: 4762680 weekly downloads on 'skills' npm package (vercel-labs/skills)
evidenceRows.notes: The 'skills' npm CLI (maintained by vercel-labs, repo: github.com/vercel-labs/skills) is the install path for mattpocock/skills via 'npx skills@latest add mattpocock/skills'. 4.76M weekly downloads on the CLI strongly implies mattpocock/skills is among the most installed suites. This is proxy-containment, not direct attribution — the CLI serves multiple contributors' suites.

evidenceRows.magnitudeRaw: ~60000 newsletter subscribers (self-reported in README)
evidenceRows.notes: README states 'Approximately 60,000 developers follow the accompanying newsletter for skill updates and new releases.' Self-reported; not independently verified, treat as soft signal.

evidenceRows.magnitudeRaw: 23917 GitHub followers on mattpocock profile
evidenceRows.notes: Author credibility signal. Matt Pocock has 23,917 GitHub followers, 222 public repos, company: Total TypeScript, blog: totaltypescript.com. Ex-Vercel, ex-Stately.

evidenceRows.magnitudeRaw: 10 active skills in skills/engineering/
evidenceRows.notes: Engineering suite contains: diagnose, grill-with-docs, improve-codebase-architecture, prototype, setup-matt-pocock-skills, tdd, to-issues, to-prd, triage, zoom-out. All confirmed present in repo tree.

evidenceRows.magnitudeRaw: 5 active skills in skills/productivity/
evidenceRows.notes: Productivity suite contains: caveman, grill-me, handoff, teach, write-a-skill. All confirmed present in repo tree.

evidenceRows.magnitudeRaw: Skill confirmed at skills/productivity/grill-me/SKILL.md
evidenceRows.notes: SKILL.md confirmed. Description: 'Interview the user relentlessly about a plan or design until reaching shared understanding, resolving each branch of the decision tree.' Type: productivity. Active and not deprecated.

evidenceRows.magnitudeRaw: Skill confirmed at skills/productivity/handoff/SKILL.md
evidenceRows.notes: SKILL.md confirmed. Generates agent-to-agent transition documents saved to OS temp dir. Redacts sensitive data. Active and not deprecated.

evidenceRows.magnitudeRaw: Skill located at skills/deprecated/ubiquitous-language/
evidenceRows.notes: ubiquitous-language is in the deprecated/ folder alongside design-an-interface, qa, request-refactor-plan. Skill still has a SKILL.md (DDD glossary extraction) but is formally deprecated by the author. GAIA registry entry should reflect deprecated status.

evidenceRows.magnitudeRaw: 2 skills in skills/personal/: edit-article, obsidian-vault
evidenceRows.notes: Personal suite is slim (2 skills). edit-article and obsidian-vault confirmed in tree. No deprecation noted.

evidenceRows.magnitudeRaw: 1125716 weekly downloads for @total-typescript/ts-reset (week of 2026-06-09 to 2026-06-15)
evidenceRows.notes: Author credibility signal via npm reach. @total-typescript/ts-reset is Matt Pocock's most prominent npm package with 1.1M+ weekly downloads, confirming he has substantial practitioner reach independent of social media. This is author-level trust signal, not direct skills package signal.

evidenceRows.magnitudeRaw: GitHub repository with 132k stars and 11.5k forks (verified on page); documents the full skills suite, grill-me, engineering, and productivity skills directly
evidenceRows.notes: Primary first-party source for the suite. Stars verified from README page fetch. Includes /grill-me, /grill-with-docs, /tdd, /diagnose, /improve-codebase-architecture, /to-prd, /to-issues, /caveman, /handoff. Newsletter cited as ~60k subscribers.

evidenceRows.magnitudeRaw: Blog post titled 'My Grill Me Skill Went Viral' on aihero.dev, updated March 23 2026; direct authorial account of viral adoption
evidenceRows.notes: Matt documents the grill-me skill's viral spread. Describes it as his most flexible skill, used for 45-minute structured AI interviews before implementation. Self-authored, first-party evidence.

evidenceRows.magnitudeRaw: Article '5 Agent Skills I Use Every Day' on aihero.dev (updated March 16 2026) — covers grill-me, /to-prd, /to-issues, /tdd, /improve-codebase-architecture directly
evidenceRows.notes: Covers exactly the skills in mattpocock/skills suite. First-party editorial by the skill author. Describes engineering and productivity workflow patterns in depth.

evidenceRows.magnitudeRaw: Article '9 Things People Get Wrong With /grill-me and /grill-with-docs' cross-posted on totaltypescript.com, updated May 25 2026
evidenceRows.notes: Published on totaltypescript.com (the flagship 40k+ developer platform) — significantly increases reach. Covers grill-me and grill-with-docs skills directly. Confirms cross-platform editorial investment in the skills framework.

evidenceRows.magnitudeRaw: Skills changelog article on aihero.dev (April 30 2026) documenting /grill-with-docs release, /diagnose, /triage; 46.6k stars cited at time of writing
evidenceRows.notes: Announces major skills reorganisation into Engineering/Productivity/Misc/Deprecated/Personal categories. Confirms ongoing active development and editorial voice around the suite.

evidenceRows.magnitudeRaw: Article 'How To Make Codebases AI Agents Love' on aihero.dev — directly addresses the engineering workflow philosophy behind the engineering skill
evidenceRows.notes: Covers the deep modules and architectural philosophy that underpins the /improve-codebase-architecture and broader engineering skills. No explicit publication date extracted, inferred 2026.

evidenceRows.magnitudeRaw: Article 'Real-world feature build with Claude Code: every step explained' on aihero.dev, published March 20 2026 — demonstrates grill-me skill in live workflow
evidenceRows.notes: Shows a real project using the grill-me skill as the first step in a Claude Code workflow. Demonstrates practical adoption of the skills suite methodology.

evidenceRows.magnitudeRaw: React Day Berlin 2023 talk 'React's Most Useful Types' — 21 minutes; Matt Pocock as headlining TypeScript educator at major international React conference
evidenceRows.notes: Confirms Matt's status as a prominent conference speaker in the TypeScript/React ecosystem. Establishes authority of the suite creator. GitNation is the primary record; exact YouTube URL not resolved. Date approximate (React Day Berlin 2023).

evidenceRows.magnitudeRaw: React Advanced 2022 talk 'TypeScript and React: Secrets of a Happy Marriage' — 21 minutes on October 24 2022; recorded and indexed on GitNation
evidenceRows.notes: Conference talk at React Advanced London 2022. Verifies Matt's standing as a TypeScript educator at significant international conferences predating the skills CLI.

evidenceRows.magnitudeRaw: React Finland 2021 talk 'Make Legacy Code Delightful with Statecharts' — 22 minutes; published August 31 2021
evidenceRows.notes: Earlier foundational conference appearance. Establishes long-standing engineering credibility underpinning the skills suite. Context predates Total TypeScript.

evidenceRows.magnitudeRaw: JS Party #326 (June 6 2024, changelog.com) — Matt Pocock cited by Eric Clemmons as a leading TypeScript expert in a build-step debate episode
evidenceRows.notes: Not a guest appearance; mentioned as an authority on TypeScript configuration and performance. His TSConfig library also cited. Demonstrates peer recognition among JS ecosystem voices.

evidenceRows.magnitudeRaw: JS Party #348 (Nov 29 2024, changelog.com) — Theo Browne identifies Matt Pocock as the archetypal TypeScript enthusiast at React Summit New York
evidenceRows.notes: Not a guest appearance. Theo's phrasing ('I'm not the TypeScript enthusiast that a Matt Pocock is') confirms Matt's widespread reputation as the leading TypeScript educator in the community.

evidenceRows.magnitudeRaw: Total TypeScript platform — 40k+ developers in community; flagship TypeScript education brand. Skills positioned as companion productivity/engineering workflows for the same audience.
evidenceRows.notes: Total TypeScript is the well-known framework/course that establishes Matt's authority and audience base. The skills suite (mattpocock/skills) is marketed to the same community. This serves as proxy-containment: the suite is implicitly endorsed by the credibility of the TotalTypeScript platform.

evidenceRows.magnitudeRaw: GitHub repository mattpocock/skills: 132,281 stars, 11,510 forks, 81 open issues, MIT licence, created 2026-02-03, last pushed 2026-06-12.
evidenceRows.notes: Verified via GitHub API. Description: 'Skills for Real Engineers. Straight from my .claude directory.' Primary author commit count: 94 of 102 total. Language: Shell 100%.

evidenceRows.magnitudeRaw: skills.sh live install badge reports 4.0M total installs for the mattpocock/skills suite. Badge SVG fetched directly from https://skills.sh/b/mattpocock/skills; text content reads 'Skills: 4.0M'.
evidenceRows.notes: skills.sh is the canonical distribution platform for Claude Code agent skills (Vercel Labs). An earlier page fetch also showed per-skill breakdowns: grill-me at 329.2k installs, improve-codebase-architecture at 269.2k, tdd at 254.1k, to-prd at 235.6k. The suite total has since crossed 4.0M.

evidenceRows.magnitudeRaw: ComposioHQ/awesome-claude-skills (64,899 stars) prominently features Matt Pocock Skills at 132k stars in a ranked table alongside Superpowers (230k), Everything Claude Code (217k), and Spec-Kit (113k). Matt Pocock is also listed among the community's key skill authors alongside Garry Tan (gstack), Andrej Karpathy, and GitHub.
evidenceRows.notes: Verified by fetching README via GitHub API and grepping for 'mattpocock'. The list shows /grill-me → /to-prd → /triage → /tdd → /diagnose → /zoom-out → /improve-codebase-architecture → /handoff as the canonical workflow. Created 2025-10-17.

evidenceRows.magnitudeRaw: shanraisshan/claude-code-best-practice (58,021 stars, 'from vibe coding to agentic engineering - practice makes claude perfect') includes mattpocock/skills in a ranked table showing 129k stars and 25 skills, alongside anthropics/skills (151k), Superpowers, and other top-tier repos.
evidenceRows.notes: Verified via GitHub API README fetch. Repo created 2025-10-31. Table row: '| [mattpocock/skills] | 129k | 25 |'

evidenceRows.magnitudeRaw: wesammustafa/Claude-Code-Everything-You-Need-to-Know (2,121 stars, 'The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code') lists mattpocock/skills as a 'Notable community skill' under 'Engineering-focused composable skills (TDD, debugging, triage). Includes the well-known `grill-me`.'
evidenceRows.notes: Verified via GitHub API. docs/skills.md contains the listing. Created 2025-08-17.

evidenceRows.magnitudeRaw: aihero.dev blog post by Matt Pocock titled 'My Grill Me Skill Went Viral', dated 2026-03-23. Describes /grill-me as 'the most flexible skill I've ever created', notes non-coding applications, and documents a recent update adding AI-recommended answers. The post title itself signals community reception.
evidenceRows.notes: Content verified via raw GitHub mirror at ulayera/ai-hero-offline. Date: March 23, 2026. aihero.dev is Matt Pocock's own publishing platform for his AI engineering newsletter (~60k subscribers per README).

evidenceRows.magnitudeRaw: aihero.dev article '5 Agent Skills I Use Every Day' (updated 2026-03-16) by Matt Pocock. Describes using /grill-me as the first step in a five-skill workflow, with Claude asking 16-50 questions per session, and references Frederick P. Brooks's 'design tree' concept as theoretical grounding.
evidenceRows.notes: Verified via raw content mirror. Also covers /to-prd, /to-issues, /tdd, and /improve-codebase-architecture.

evidenceRows.magnitudeRaw: aiskillstore/marketplace (361 stars, 'Security-audited skills for Claude, Codex & Claude Code') contains a full security audit of grill-me completed 2026-03-27 using analysis_version 3.0.0. Risk level: safe, is_blocked: false, safe_to_publish: true. Includes complete skill metadata, FAQ, use cases, and output examples.
evidenceRows.notes: Verified via GitHub API. All 29 mattpocock skills have individual skill-report.json entries in this marketplace. The audit notes a static-analyser false positive on 'weak cryptographic algorithm' but clears the skill as purely conversational.

evidenceRows.magnitudeRaw: vinvcn/mattpocock-skills-zh-CN: Simplified Chinese localization of mattpocock/skills with 681 stars and 72 forks, created 2026-04-28. Karasukaigan/skills-zh: Japanese/Chinese localization with 165 stars. amazingloft999-droid/mattpocock-skills: English mirror with 234 stars.
evidenceRows.notes: Three independent high-quality localization/mirror repositories demonstrate broad international adoption. vinvcn is the most starred localization fork.

evidenceRows.magnitudeRaw: aihero.dev changelog article 'Skills Changelog: Ubiquitous Language -> /grill-with-docs' (published 2026-04-30) documents the deprecation of /ubiquitous-language and its consolidation into /grill-with-docs. Explains the evolution toward CONTEXT.md and multiple bounded contexts (DDD pattern).
evidenceRows.notes: Verified via raw content mirror at ulayera/ai-hero-offline. Confirms /ubiquitous-language was a live, used skill before supersession — historical provenance evidence.

evidenceRows.magnitudeRaw: aihero.dev skill page for /handoff (updated 2026-05-13) with full documentation: installation, workflow description, ideal use cases, and synergy with /prototype, /to-prd, /to-issues.
evidenceRows.notes: Verified via raw content mirror. Install command: npx skills add mattpocock/skills skill=handoff -y -g. Confirms handoff is a live, maintained skill.

evidenceRows.magnitudeRaw: aihero.dev skills hub page (dated with changelogs from 2026-04-30 and 2026-05-11) lists all engineering skills: /grill-with-docs, /to-prd, /to-issues, /tdd, /handoff, /prototype, /improve-codebase-architecture, /triage, /review. Tagline: 'a practical skill system for engineers who want to use AI'.
evidenceRows.notes: Verified by direct fetch of aihero.dev/skills. This is Matt Pocock's authoritative documentation for his engineering skill category.

evidenceRows.magnitudeRaw: Chinese blog post 'Agent Skills for Real Engineers — mattpocock-skills' on houbb.github.io published 2026-04-27, documenting the full skill list, installation, and use cases across planning, development, tooling, and knowledge management.
evidenceRows.notes: Verified by fetching the page. Third-party Chinese-language documentation and endorsement, consistent with the broader Chinese developer adoption seen in the 681-star localization fork.

evidenceRows.magnitudeRaw: Korean blog post 'Claude Code Skills 22개 한국어 정리' on jkf87.github.io (2026-04-27) covers the full mattpocock/skills suite in Korean, featuring /grill-me with translation '내 계획을 끝까지 추궁해라' (thoroughly challenges planning assumptions).
evidenceRows.notes: Verified by fetching the page. Third-party Korean-language documentation, indicating adoption in the Korean developer community.

evidenceRows.magnitudeRaw: travisvn/awesome-claude-skills (13,515 stars) references Anthropic's engineering deep-dive 'Equipping Agents with Skills' alongside mattpocock/skills as a community example repository. The Anthropic article (2025-10-16, authors: Barry Zhang, Keith Lazuka, Mahesh Murag) defines the SKILL.md standard that mattpocock/skills implements.
evidenceRows.notes: The Anthropic article itself does not name mattpocock/skills, but travisvn/awesome-claude-skills places the Anthropic engineering post and mattpocock/skills in the same resource section. mattpocock/skills is one of the most prominent community implementations of the standard.
